purpose: Bloom ground chile in warm fat and simmer a tomato base to build a stable, deeply aromatic sauce foundation.

steps.instruction: Warm olive oil over medium-low heat.
steps.sensory_cue: Oil looks glossy and fluid but does not shimmer aggressively.
steps.why: Moderate heat protects volatile aromatic compounds from scorching.

steps.instruction: Add smoked paprika and whisk continuously.
steps.sensory_cue: Aroma shifts from dusty/raw to sweet-smoky without dark specks.
steps.why: Short blooming extracts fat-soluble flavor molecules while avoiding bitter burn.

steps.instruction: Add crushed tomatoes and stir to fully disperse the spice phase.
steps.sensory_cue: Color becomes uniform brick red with no dry spice pockets.
steps.why: Even dispersion prevents hot spots and creates a cohesive emulsion-like sauce texture.

steps.instruction: Simmer gently, stirring periodically until the sauce thickens.
steps.sensory_cue: Sauce coats the back of a spoon and leaves a clear line when swiped.
steps.why: Controlled reduction concentrates flavor and sets a stable viscosity.

failure_modes.symptom: Sauce tastes bitter and appears dark brown early.
failure_modes.prevention: Use medium-low heat and keep spices moving constantly.
failure_modes.recovery: Restart bloom phase and fold in the affected batch incrementally if salvageable.

failure_modes.cause: Insufficient reduction time or excessive heat fluctuations.
failure_modes.prevention: Maintain gentle simmer and verify spoon-coating cue before finishing.
failure_modes.recovery: Continue reducing in 3-minute intervals, stirring and rechecking viscosity.

sensory_checkpoints: Bloomed spice aroma is sweet-smoky, not acrid.
Tomato-spice mixture is uniform in color.
Final sauce coats spoon with stable sheen.

variations.name: Smoky Mornay-style base
variations.delta: Whisk in grated aged cheese off-heat for a richer gratin-ready sauce.

variations.name: Roasted allium variant
variations.delta: Fold in roasted garlic puree before final reduction.

cultural_context: Technique blends sofrito-style blooming with contemporary catering sauce consistency standards.
notes: Keep paprika bloom short; most failures happen in this window.
If scaling up, widen pan surface area before increasing burner power.

lineage.note: Inspired by traditional sofrito blooming methods.

modifications: Reduced bloom window to 20-30 seconds to reduce bitterness risk.

history.author: UMF editorial
history.notes: Pedagogy-first technique card aligned to updated technique schema.
